title: pungent turkish figs tagine
description: a pungent, pillowy, lazy-sunday turkish dish featuring figs and a rich, savory sauce. this vegetarian tagine is perfect for a buddhist temple-friendly sunday meal, served with fluffy rice or warm pita bread.

ingredients.name: figs, dried
ingredients.notes: any variety, but preferably sweet or Turkish

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: use a mild, non-extra virgin oil to avoid overpowering the figs

ingredients.name: onion
ingredients.notes: any variety, but preferably sweet or red onion

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: use 2 for the sauce and 2 for the glaze

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use a flaky sea salt or kosher salt

ingredients.name: honey
ingredients.notes: use a mild, pure honey or alternative sweetener

ingredients.name: chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: use fresh, not dried or frozen

instructions: heat the olive oil in the bottom of a large, heavy tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
add the sliced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until it's softened and lightly caramelized, about 20 minutes.
add the minced garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes, until it's fragrant and lightly toasted.
add the cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute, until the spices are fragrant.
add the dried figs and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes, until they're rehydrated and the sauce is syrupy.
add the lemon juice and honey, and stir to combine.
transfer the sauce to a blender or food processor and blend until smooth.
return the sauce to the tagine or Dutch oven and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes, until it's thickened and reduced slightly.
taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
transfer the tagine to a serving dish and garnish with chopped parsley.
serve warm or at room temperature, with fluffy rice or warm pita bread.
